meta.title: CASEMEX — Mexican green coffee for export
meta.description: Mexican green coffee trader and exporter since 1996. A family-run company from Chiapas supplying traders, exporters, roasters and brands with steady volume, consistent quality and traceability.
meta.ogAlt: CASEMEX — Mexican green coffee, beans pouring from a jute sack

hero.kicker: Mexican green coffee for export · Since 1996
hero.headline: The coffee that lets you deliver, every time
hero.subhead: A family company from Chiapas. We give roasters, brands and exporters the steady volume, consistent quality and on-time delivery their business depends on.

historia.title: A family-run company from Chiapas, since 1996
historia.lead: Three generations doing the same thing: reading every harvest and standing behind what we ship.
historia.p1: We started in 1996, in Chiapas, buying coffee from the region's growers and learning to read each harvest. Three decades later we are still a family business, now in its third generation, with more than 100 people on the team and an agronomy crew working in the field, alongside the people who grow the coffee.
historia.p2: Today we are one of Mexico's largest coffee traders, but the way we work has not changed: decisions made close to the source, direct relationships, and the responsibility of standing behind every lot we ship.

proceso.title: From the mountains to the world
proceso.lead: We follow the coffee through every stage, from the high-altitude farm to the bag ready for export.

steps.title: The highlands
steps.caption: Chiapas coffee grows on high slopes, where the bean ripens slowly and gains its character.

steps.title: The grower
steps.caption: Our agronomy team works with hundreds of growers on their own land, harvest after harvest.

steps.title: Processing
steps.caption: Washed, naturals and European and American preps: each profile is handled with a defined method.

steps.title: Consolidation
steps.caption: We bring lots from many hands into even volumes, with traceability back to origin.

steps.title: Green coffee
steps.caption: It ships graded, bagged and ready for roasters and exporters in any country.

productos.title: Green coffee of Mexican origin
productos.lead: Grades and preparations for every destination. The spec sheet makes the difference, not the photo.

arabica.name: Arabica
arabica.intro: Our core bean, grown at altitude. Available in different grades and preparations depending on your destination and the profile you need.
grades: Strictly High Grown (SHG) and High Grown
Naturals and Washed — Prime and Good

robusta.name: Robusta
robusta.intro: Mexican Robusta for blends and volume, in conventional and certified versions. More body and caffeine.

productos.note: Spec sheets with profile, altitude and certification are available as a PDF for each product.

certificaciones.title: Certifications that back every lot
certificaciones.lead: We work with coffee certified to the standards your clients recognize.

items.name: USDA Organic
items.blurb: Coffee grown without synthetic agrochemicals, under the United States organic standard.

items.name: OCIA
items.blurb: International organic certification that verifies crop management from the field.

items.name: 4C
items.blurb: A baseline standard confirming sustainable social, environmental and economic practices.

items.name: Fair Trade
items.blurb: Fair trade: terms and prices that sustain growers' income.

certificaciones.note: We display the official seals once verified with the client; never an approximate logo.

diferenciadores.title: What changes when you work with us
diferenciadores.lead: Your biggest risk is not getting the coffee you need. Here we turn it into certainty.

blocks.title: Volume you can count on
blocks.body: A consolidated network of hundreds of growers means the volume is there when you need it, not only in a good harvest.

blocks.title: The same quality, lot after lot
blocks.body: We grade and check every shipment so the profile you approved is the one you get next time.

blocks.title: You know where it comes from
blocks.body: Every lot arrives with traceability to origin and the certification your market requires.

blocks.title: One partner, not hundreds
blocks.body: Instead of coordinating with hundreds of scattered growers, you deal with one company that decides fast and stands by its word.

origenes.title: Our regions in Chiapas
origenes.lead: We source across the state's coffee-growing zones, each with its own altitude, climate and cup profile.
origenes.mapTitle: Map of the coffee-growing zones of Chiapas where CASEMEX sources green coffee

regions.name: Soconusco
regions.note: Pacific coast, high-grown coffee beside the Sierra Madre.

regions.name: Sierra Madre
regions.note: High slopes and cool climate, clean and sweet profiles.

regions.name: Frailesca
regions.note: Central valleys and hillsides with volume production.

regions.name: Norte / Selva
regions.note: Humid zones in the north of the state, shade-grown coffee.

origenes.note: We'll confirm the exact zones and their profiles with you before every operation.

contacto.title: Request your quote
contacto.lead: Tell us what coffee you need and we'll come back with a proposal. You can also reach us on WhatsApp.

form.name: Name
form.successBody: We'll be in touch soon, during business hours.
form.errorBody: Please try again or message us on WhatsApp.

footer.legalName: Cafés y Semillas de México S.A. de C.V.
footer.blurb: Mexican green coffee trader and exporter since 1996.

imagineAlt.arabica: Green Arabica coffee in a jute sack
